text.title: Requirements Prioritization Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the requirements-prioritization methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale and is referenced from `content/06-decision-tree.xml`.

rule.statement: Step 1 (lock method): Pick MoSCoW/RICE/Kano/WSJF; lock and version-stamp.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the lock method gate.

rule.statement: Step 2 (elicit weights): Per-stakeholder weight vector; reconcile to group.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the elicit weights gate.

rule.statement: Step 3 (score): Apply method scoring; couple value with effort.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the score gate.

rule.statement: Step 4 (distribution check): Verify non-flat distribution; force redistribution if needed.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the distribution check gate.

rule.statement: Step 5 (publish record): Compose PR record with rationale + sign-off.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the publish record gate.

rule.statement: If preconditions for requirements-prioritization do not hold, do not apply this methodology; route to an alternative as the decision tree suggests.
rule.rationale: Methodologies misapplied to wrong contexts create overhead without value; explicit skip preserves the agent's budget.
